title: allium paniculatum subsp. rupestre
intro: Allium paniculatum subsp. rupestre, also known as Korean mountain onion or wild garlic, is a species of wild onion native to mountainous regions of Korea. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antibacterial and antifungal properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Allium paniculatum subsp. rupestre include reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system, among others. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or dried powder to be used in various herbal preparations, such as supplements and topical creams.
